title: AMC Entertainment Q3 2024 Earnings Summary
text:
AMC reported mixed results for the third quarter of 2024. Total revenues reached $1,348.8 million, representing a 31% increase from Q2 2024. However, the company posted a net loss of $20.7 million, though this was a significant improvement—narrowing by 37% compared to the previous quarter's loss.

Adjusted EBITDA came in at $161.8 million, described as the second-best performance of any third quarter in AMC's 104-year history and four times stronger than Q2 2024's figure.

BALANCE SHEET IMPROVEMENTS
CEO Adam Aron highlighted progress on debt management: our debt transactions announcement in July pushed the maturity of $2.4 billion of our long term debt out into the future for several years, from 2026 to 2029 and 2030.

Year-to-date, AMC reduced outstanding debt by $349 million through capital markets activities. Cash on hand stood at $527.4 million at quarter's end.

OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS
The company achieved record third-quarter admissions revenue per patron and all-time record food and beverage revenue per patron. Notably, AMC matched pre-pandemic Q3 2019 Adjusted EBITDA levels despite 25% lower attendance than that period.

FORWARD OUTLOOK
Management expressed optimism about upcoming theatrical releases, expecting industry-wide box office growth through 2026.
